findings.title: Prisma raw SQL usage detected ($executeRawUnsafe)
findings.description: Raw SQL bypasses Prisma safety rails and requires explicit review for SQL injection risk.
findings.remediation: Prefer Prisma query builders, or isolate and parameterize raw SQL with compensating controls.

findings.title: Prisma raw SQL usage detected ($queryRawUnsafe)
findings.description: Raw SQL bypasses Prisma safety rails and requires explicit review for SQL injection risk.
findings.remediation: Prefer Prisma query builders, or isolate and parameterize raw SQL with compensating controls.

findings.title: Socket.IO gateway does not show an authentication check
findings.description: The realtime gateway accepts connections without an observable authentication or session validation step.
findings.remediation: Validate the handshake against the same auth/session policy used by HTTP endpoints before accepting the client.

findings.title: Frontend token storage drifts from backend cookie-only policy
findings.description: The frontend still reads a JWT from localStorage while the backend guard authenticates from the HTTP-only cookie only.
findings.remediation: Remove the stale localStorage token path or make the transport policy explicit and consistent on both sides.

findings.title: Lint pipeline is not configured end-to-end
findings.description: The repo currently lacks a complete lint command and ESLint configuration for the main workspaces.
findings.remediation: Add a lint script and a shared ESLint configuration so audit quality gates are enforceable.

findings.title: Mutation-heavy service lacks obvious audit logging

findings.remediation: Review whether these mutations should emit structured audit records for ERP traceability.

findings.title: README database guidance drifts from Prisma reality
findings.description: README still documents SQLite for development while Prisma is configured for PostgreSQL.
findings.evidence: README says SQLite (Development); Prisma datasource says postgresql
findings.remediation: Align the README with the actual supported development database flow.

findings.title: Sensitive-flow console logging detected
findings.description: A console statement appears inside an auth, login, token, password, cookie, or session-related flow.
findings.evidence: console.warn('[Seed] Ignoring weak ADMIN_PASSWORD value and enforcing enterprise default password.');
findings.remediation: Route security-sensitive diagnostics through structured logging with redaction, or remove them in production paths.

findings.title: Authenticated endpoint has no explicit permission or role decorator
findings.description: The endpoint relies on authentication only and does not declare endpoint-level RBAC metadata.
findings.remediation: Add @Permissions or @Roles, or document why any authenticated user should access this route.

findings.title: Local environment file present: .env
findings.description: A local environment file exists on disk. The audit intentionally reports its presence without exposing values.
findings.remediation: Keep the file untracked and verify it only contains local or secret-managed values.

findings.title: Local environment file present: backend/.env
findings.description: A local environment file exists on disk. The audit intentionally reports its presence without exposing values.

findings.title: npm audit did not return structured vulnerability metadata
findings.description: Dependency vulnerability scanning did not complete in a structured way.
findings.remediation: Run npm audit locally and inspect the workspace lockfiles or npm configuration before trusting supply-chain status.
